stages.heading: Stardate 2400.0 (Introduction)
commentary: Welcome to an unexpected sequence of events. While enjoying a breather in between publishing playthroughs; including, perhaps, stockpiling a few extra; a new adaptation was ready to be played.

This is our first adventure with Space Imperia 4X. While it is still undergoing development at the time of this playthrough's publishing, we opt to try our hand against its unique AI system. This is a semi-blind playthrough, in the sense that we know virtually none of this new system, sans its existence.
A limited subset of the advanced pre-Close Encounters rules are available in this build. Minelayers and Minesweepers are available, but not pipelines/fighters/cloaking.

stages.heading: Stardate 2400.3
commentary: As this playthrough showcases, both players take a similar opening. We are playing as Red, and have been randomly assigned the initiative.
Already the AI has ran into a snag, having uncovered a black hole right away. Their lone ship rolled a 10, sucking it in.

stages.heading: Stardate 2400.6
commentary: Already here is where the paths differ. It appears that their AI is aggressive, but has a few limitations of its own. Probably to be expected from Normal difficulty, the only setting available in the current pre-release [demo] build.
We exposed our own black hole in other news, but our Scout rolled a 4 (survived).

stages.heading: Stardate 2400.9
commentary: The respective black holes grant us a considerable advantage in uncovering the rest of our home systems.

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.0
commentary: Here is where things operate differently. Because competitive games typically hide most information, we know not most of the AI's economic phase. Income and (at phase's end) bids are public information; which is why we can still see those figures on the production table. Their technology will also likely be inaccurate, as it updates as we learn of their studies.

Anyway, we build 1 Scout (-6) and 2 Colony Ships (-16). This process spends all of our money. We can also see that the AI, dubbed Breich Alpha, built 2 colony ships themselves. Since neither player bidded, we can choose who takes the initiative. We will keep it for now.

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.2
commentary: Since our home system black hole is known, there is no risk left to send in multiple ships per unexplored home system hex.

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.5
commentary: It appears we have an edge case that requires clarification: Namely, sending a colony ship to unexplored space together with a combat ship

stages.heading: Stardate 2401.8
commentary: We got our answer! It is possible to colonize a planet that was just explored, just as we previously inferred when recording our playthroughs. (We simply initiated colonization during the economic phase.)

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.0
commentary: About the same income so far. We go on ahead and buy 2 more Colony Ships (-16) and an extra Ship Yard (-6) at the homeworld. Breich Alpha also builds 2 colony ships.
Just like before, neither player placed a bid. We will keep the initiative.

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.1
commentary: Competitive scenarios reward aggression, but we need to be wary of any counters they may attempt. Since we are almost out of unexplored home systems, we will go on ahead and begin venturing out into deep space.
And of course we would reveal the first Danger marker! There goes our first scout...

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.4
commentary: Two Danger markers back-to-back! This is bringing back some memories of pre-Replicators deep space mixes.

stages.heading: Stardate 2402.7
commentary: Not the best situation, but it could be worse. We can always build bigger ships, once we have a clear pathway...

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.0
commentary: Soon, we will have an economy advantage, though it will likely only last so long with Terraforming being unlikely to be useful to research... Still, we buy Ship Size 2 (-10), followed by 1 Colony Ship (-8) and another homeworld Ship Yard (-6).
Breich Alpha builds 1 more Colony Ship and 1 (or more) unidentified combat ships. As usual, no bids take place, so we will keep the initiative.

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.3
commentary: Those are some weird moves Breich Alpha is making. Granted, we probably are doing them no favors by taking the first steps into deep space.

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.6
commentary: Evidently, Breich Alpha did not like the pressure we are applying to their home systems. One of their colony ships is also acting suspicious. Could they have developed Terraforming 1?

stages.heading: Stardate 2403.9
commentary: We will take advantage of the nearby asteroid belt, and use it to negate any Weapon Class or Attack equipment advantage they may leverage.

Sure enough, their newest fleet, revealed to be a lone Destroyer, has engaged our Scout in battle. Since we have the Tactics advantage, we go first. We rolled a 2 (hit ≤3), destroying their newest ship and securing our first battle victory!

stages.heading: Stardate 2404.0
commentary: And here is where issues with the pre-release build arise with economics. We expected to be able to carry the 5 CP that we did not use last economic phase (≤30) and to be able to unload 2 mineral counters in a single set of turns. But, for some reason, our carryover zeroed out; and one of the mineral counters did not count. (Did it get deleted? Or was it delayed?)

Interim feedback suggests we may have triggered bizarre edge/corner case(s) with Space Imperia 4X. Considering that we built Replay Center to be as powerful as it is, we understand how difficult it can be to pinpoint the exact origin of these cases.

Somewhat fortunately for us, this snafu barely did not spoil us out of our most crucial spending: Ship Size 3 (-15) and 2 Cruisers (-24). However, it does deny us the ability to consider placing a bid to defend our initiative.
Yet... it could be worse. Breich Alpha built an unknonwn number of combat ships... including something possibly large at Orion (H5); but still set a bid of 0. We will keep the initiative.

stages.heading: Stardate 2404.2 (The Battle for Sector G5)
commentary: Moving right along, we consolidate our 2 Scouts together, making our choke point at least twice as strong.
One of their starting scouts hits up our group. We screen no ships, and gain the Fleet Size Bonus. Our Scouts then rolled 1,7 (1 hit ≤4)

Meanwhile, Breich Alpha got greedy for broadening the known deep space separating our two home systems... and paid for it with a Danger marker. Most likely though, this was the last starting scout they lost.

stages.heading: Stardate 2404.5
commentary: And now Breich Alpha thinks its clever by bringing their Miner towards our home systems, in an attempt to steal some minerals. We can easily plug the gap though.

stages.heading: Stardate 2404.8
commentary: Overall, this was a nice set of turns.

stages.heading: Stardate 2405.0
commentary: And now Space Imperia 4X's mathematics suddenly catch up... exactly, we may add. We suspect that one/both of our minerals the phase before was delayed, with the possibility that it treats our unspent CP as minerals.

Since we may still have to conduct battles in the deep space asteroid belt, we start with Tactics 1 (-15). We also buy 3 Cruisers (-36). Finally, not wanting to take a chance, we bid 2 CP to try to defend the initiative.
Breich Alpha places some more unknown combat ships. They still bid 0, though we now find that hard to believe, as the client was displaying our bid of 0.

stages.heading: Stardate 2405.1 (The Battle for Sector G4)
commentary: We combine our oldest forces and unveil the combat ships at the hex we pincired... 1 Destroyer. We screen no ships, and our Cruisers rolled 5,1 (2 hits ≤5), cleaning it up easily.

stages.heading: Stardate 2405.4
commentary: Bizarrely, they have made no attempt to preserve the Colony Ship that was still in space. Perhaps they were using them in an effort to take our last planets. Still, our oldest fleet intercepted it... and destroyed it without fail.

stages.heading: Stardate 2405.7
commentary: We split our scouts again in order to broaden a blockade. Against Eden (H3), our ships rolled 3,6/6 (1 hit ≤4/3). Against Rigel (I3), our lone Scout rolled a 2 (hit ≤2).

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.0
commentary: No hiccups seem to remain with respect to mathematics. Blockading two of their colonies gives us a considerable income advantage. We buy another 3 Cruisers, and then bid another 2 to try to fortify the initiative.
Breich Alpha builds more unknown ships at J3 and J5; and appears to place a bid of 0.

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.3 (The Battle for Eden)
commentary: Player Turn
Since one path has become more clearer than the other, we opt to just eliminate the Miner that was previously sitting next to us. Our ships rolled 8,8/2,8 for bombardment (1 hit ≤4/3).
Computer Turn
Their newest forces, revealed to be 2 Destroyers + 2 Scouts, have come to defend their beseiged colony.
1. Round 1: Our Cruisers have the best weapon class, so we go first. We rolled 6,9 against their destroyers (0 hits ≤4)
• Their Destroyers rolled 8,8 against our cruisers (0 hits ≤3)
• Our Scouts rolled 1,9 (1 hit ≤3)
• Their Scouts rolled 6,8 against our cruisers (0 hits ≤2)
• Round 2: Our Cruisers rolled a 1 against their last destroyer, followed by a 2 against their scouts (2 hits ≤4)
• Our Scouts rolled 7,2 (1 hit ≤3), destroying their fleet

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.6 (The Bombardment of Eden)
commentary: Our bombardment spree continues. We rolled 1,7/7,9 (1 hit ≤4/3).

stages.heading: Stardate 2406.9 (The Battle for Eden)
commentary: Player Turn
While we finish up bombardment, we exploit Breich Alpha's... confidence with their colony ship. We rolled 7,9/1,1 (2 hits ≤4/3), destroying their colony.
Computer Turn

They send in their newest ships (revealed to be 1 Destroyer) to intercept our reinforcing cruisers, but not until their Colony Ship was already destroyed. We screen no ships, and then rolled 7,2,2 (2 hits ≤5), eliminating the tardy straggler.

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.0
commentary: It appears an edge case rears its ugly head once more. Our sole Miner managed to unload two (2) more mineral deposits in the previous set of turns, but we apparently got credit for neither of them. This time, our efforts to pinpoint it has allowed the Space Imperia 4X team to track down the source.

The incident spoils our efforts to buy Defense 1, but we will carry on with 3 more Cruisers (-36). Since it appears we have eliminiated the last of the mobile combat ships, we will place a bid of 0 this time.
Confusingly, Breich Alpha buys 2 colony ships of all things! And places a bid of 0. We will keep the initiative, still.

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.2 (The Bombardment of Rigel)
commentary: This has certainly been a bizarre adventure, but to be expected when playing around with pre-release builds.
Against their colony of Rigel, we rolled 4,4,6,7,9/4,5 (2 hits ≤4/3), damaging it, but not destroying it.

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.5
commentary: Confident in their vulnerability, we opt to go for the <q>throat</q>, and threaten their homeworld.

stages.heading: Stardate 2407.8 (The Battle for Terra)
commentary: The time has come to assault Terra, their homeworld. They got 6 Ship Yards to back it up, but this is not a good sign when we got a total of 7 ships (12 hull sizes worth).
1. Round 1: Some of our Cruisers have the Tactics advantage, so these go first. We rolled 1,6,10 (1 hit ≤4)
• Their Ship Yards rolled 2,7,7,1,10 against the cruisers that have Tactics 0 (2 hits ≤2), destroying one of them
• Our last Tactics 0 Cruiser rolls a 4 (hit ≤4)
• Our 2 Scouts rolled 1,9 (1 hit ≤3)
• Round 2: Our newer Cruisers rolled 1,1,7 (1 hit ≤4)
• Their last Ship Yard rolled an 8 against the cruiser that have Tactics 0 (≤2 to hit)
• Our oldest Cruiser rolls a 2 (hit ≤4), finishing it off
• Bombardment Round: Our ships rolled 2,2,8,9/9,9 (2 hits ≤4/3)
Additionally, our incoming Cruisers at Rigel (I3) rolled 3,4,4 (3 hits ≤4), glassing this colony also.

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.0
commentary: And now the economic figures catch up again. We have also found a quirk that allows us to build a maximum of 2 Cruisers (even if we stack them). We will instead buy Ship Size 4 (-20), and begin building 3 Battlecruisers (-45).
Breich Alpha is extremely vulnerable, now that their homeworld is exposed, and they probably have no Ship Yards left in play. It also appears they did not bother building Ship Yard(s) somewhere else.

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.1 (The Bombardment of Terra)
commentary: And now the acid test becomes: How quickly can we finish off Terra? Our ships rolled 4,5,7,8/1,3 (3 hits ≤4/3), finishing off this homeworld exactly!

stages.heading: Stardate 2408.4 (Conclusion)
commentary: And thus ends this early adventure of Space Imperia 4X. Definitely was not easy working around the rough edges, but this is to be an expectation of a pre-release build. Still, taking the time to do a pre-recording for the Replay Center has given us an advantage that might have otherwise been missed, with respect to Miners unloading 2 mineral counters in a single economic phase's worth of turns.

We may still do more pre-release playthroughs, but probably will not convert them to the Replay Center like we did this one. Especially since subsequent testing tends to carry <q>diminishing returns</q>.
